models.code:
class Customer:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String)
    credit_limit = Column(DECIMAL)  # Credit limit for each customer
    balance = Column(DECIMAL)  # Balance derived from order sums
models.description: Model representing a Customer with an auto-generated ID, name, credit limit, and a derived balance attribute.
models.name: Customer

models.code:
class Order:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customer.id'))
    notes = Column(String)
    date_shipped = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)  # Nullable to indicate not shipped yet
    amount_total = Column(DECIMAL)  # Derived attribute from item sums
models.description: Model representing an Order with a relationship back to a Customer, a notes field, and a derived amount total attribute.
models.name: Order

models.code:
class Item:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('order.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('product.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    unit_price = Column(DECIMAL)  # Copied from Product.unit_price
    amount = Column(DECIMAL)  # Derived attribute from quantity and unit price
models.description: Model representing an Item associated with an Order and Product, with derived amount attribute based on quantity and unit price.
models.name: Item

models.code:
class Product:
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String)
    unit_price = Column(DECIMAL)
models.description: Model for representing Products with a Product ID, name, and unit price.
models.name: Product

rules.name: CustomerBalanceBelowCreditLimit
rules.description: Ensure the balance of a customer is less than the credit limit.
rules.code: Rule.constraint(validate=Customer, as_condition=lambda row: row.balance <= row.credit_limit, error_msg='Customer balance ({row.balance}) exceeds credit limit ({row.credit_limit})')

rules.name: CustomerBalanceDerivedFromOrders
rules.description: The Customer's balance is derived from the sum of their unshipped orders.
rules.code: Rule.sum(derive=Customer.balance, as_sum_of=Order.amount_total, where=lambda row: row.date_shipped is None)

rules.name: OrderAmountTotalSumOfItems
rules.description: An Order's amount total is derived from the sum of its items.

rules.name: ItemAmountDerivedFromQuantityAndUnitPrice
rules.description: Derive the item amount from the quantity and unit_price.

rules.name: SendOrderToKafka
rules.description: Send the Order to Kafka topic 'order_shipping' if the date_shipped is not None.
rules.code: Rule.after_flush_row_event(on_class=Order, calling=kafka_producer.send_row_to_kafka, if_condition=lambda row: row.date_shipped is not None, with_args={'topic': 'order_shipping'})

test_data: Four entities were created with four rows each, given the importance of test data in ensuring that the model was accurately represented and well tested. Each row was carefully crafted to reflect the relevant entities.

test_data_rows.code: customer1 = Customer(id=1, name='John Doe', credit_limit=Decimal('5000.00'), balance=Decimal('0.00'))
order1 = Order(id=1, customer_id=1, notes='First Order', date_shipped=None, amount_total=Decimal('0.00'))
item1 = Item(id=1, order_id=1, product_id=1, quantity=2, unit_price=Decimal('20.00'), amount=Decimal('40.00'))

test_data_sqlite: INSERT INTO customer (id, name, credit_limit, balance) VALUES (1, 'John Doe', 5000.00, 0.00); INSERT INTO order (id, customer_id, notes, date_shipped, amount_total) VALUES (1, 1, 'First Order', NULL, 0.00); INSERT INTO item (id, order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, amount) VALUES (1, 1, 1, 2, 20.00, 40.00); INSERT INTO product (id, name, unit_price) VALUES (1, 'Product A', 20.00);
name: CustomerOrderSystem
